params.name: Oersted's Compass
params.description:
On Apr 9, 1820 AD, the Danish Scientist, Oersted discovered while teaching a class, that an electric current can deflect a magnetic needle.
This compass was very significant in Oersted's research. Oersted was a physicist known for the discovery of the magnetic effect of electric currents. One day, on a whim, he placed a magnetic needle nearby a current-carrying wire. At that moment, he was surprised to find that the magnetic needle jumped.
